raw_response: I agree with the final conclusion that the Frankel appliance is the only tissue-borne functional appliance. One key aspect that reinforces this view is the unique nature of how the Frankel appliance engages primarily with soft tissues without requiring any physical connection to skeletal structures. Unlike other appliances discussed, where there might be indirect influence on bone or reliance on hardware embedded in the jaws, the Frankel specifically leverages the natural musculature and soft tissues of the face and mouth for its therapeutic effect. This makes it stand apart as truly tissue-borne. Disagreement would imply another option could also fit these criteria perfectly, but none have been shown to match the Frankel's complete lack of need for hard tissue support mechanisms. Answer choice remains A.
